channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump’s Iran Approach Sows Confusion
item.description: President Trump’s pendulum swings on Iran have often seemed driven by mood and moment rather than any discernible strategy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump’s shifts reflect a political tug of war between hawkish supporters urging him to hit Iran harder and noninterventionists urging him to make a quick deal.

item.title: A Draft U.S.-Iran Plan Is Said to Be on the Table. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials say they are closing in on the terms of a preliminary agreement. Yet sticking points, particularly over the Strait of Hormuz, remain.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran depicting the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in May.

item.title: Israel to Cut Ties With U.N. Chief Over Its Inclusion on Sexual Violence Blacklist
item.description: The United Nations secretary general, António Guterres, told Israel there had been an “increasing number of cases” of sexual violence against Palestinian detainees, according to a letter shared by Israel’s U.N. ambassador.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations, Danny Danon, at U.N. headquarters in March.

item.title: In Strait of Hormuz Standoff, U.S. and Iran Deploy Economic Warfare
item.description: The American blockade and Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz have created a stalemate that is neither peace nor raging conflict, with both sides attempting economic strangulation on the water.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels anchored at the Strait of Hormuz on Monday. Control of the waterway is a tool Iran has used as leverage against the United States.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Israel Will Control 70 Percent of Gaza, Squeezing Hamas
item.description: Israel’s military continues to conduct strikes and seize territory despite a cease-fire with Hamas. Many of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s supporters see the war as unfinished business.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Destruction in downtown Gaza City on Thursday.

item.title: The U.S. re-sanctioned a U.N. legal expert critical of Israel.
item.description: Just a few days ago, a U.S. court ordered the Treasury Department to suspend its penalties on Francesca Albanese, whose work on Israel has drawn accusations of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Francesca Albanese, the U.N.’s special rapporteur for the Israeli-occupied West Bank and Gaza, in March.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Move Toward Agreement to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has yet to sign off on an extension of the cease-fire that would allow the two sides to negotiate nuclear and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan, Tyler Pager and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A drone image shows vessels anchored on Monday in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut, Expanding Offensive in Lebanon
item.description: It was the first attack by Israel on the Lebanese capital in weeks and could complicate efforts by the United States and Iran to agree to a peace deal to end the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers at the scene of a strike in Choueifat, Lebanon, south of Beirut, on Thursday.

item.title: The Mideast Is Baffled by Trump’s Call to Expand Abraham Accords
item.description: The president said more countries should be required to recognize Israel as part of a deal to end the war with Iran. Analysts say the chances of that happening are close to zero.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Isabel Kershner and Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump with leaders from Israel, Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates at the signing of the Abraham Accords in Washington in 2020.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan, Tyler Pager, David E. Sanger, Eric Schmitt and Alan Yuhas

item.title: U.S. Strikes Military Sites in Iran for Second Time in 3 Days
item.description: As a potential cease-fire hangs in the balance, an official said the strikes were in self-defense, with targets including four one-way attack drones launched by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships in the water near the Iranian port city of Bandar Abbas earlier this month.

item.title: Trump Threatens Oman Over the Strait, Then Says, ‘They’ll Be Fine.’
item.description: President Trump said there would be serious repercussions if Iran and Oman move forward with a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters at the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Says He Feels No Political Pressure to Make an Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump held out hope for a peace agreement, but said high oil prices would not force his hand.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Erika Solomon, Euan Ward, Luke Broadwater and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump meeting with his cabinet at the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: To Make a Deal With Trump, Iran Wants Billions of Its Money Unfrozen
item.description: The money has become a sticking point in talks, with Iran insisting that meaningful negotiations cannot begin without the funds’ release.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A crowd carrying a photo of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, during a ceremony in Tehran on Sunday. The Iranian government has been pummeled economically by Western sanctions and recent wars.

item.title: Lebanese Resigned to a Long War, Even if U.S. and Iran Make a Deal
item.description: Few in Lebanon believe that diplomatic efforts to end the wider war will bring peace to their country, as clashes between Israel and Hezbollah intensify in the south.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A resident assesses the damage in the aftermath of an Israeli airstrike in Tyre, Lebanon, Tuesday.

item.title: What Iranian State Media Says Is in Outline of ‘Unofficial’ Deal With U.S.
item.description: The White House immediately dismissed the report, which detailed terms for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, as a “complete fabrication.”
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Sanam Mahoozi and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to be a top negotiating point between the Trump administration and Iranian officials.

item.title: Israel Continues Its Escalation in Lebanon With New Evacuation Warnings
item.description: The Israeli military issued sweeping evacuation warnings for two southern cities, indicating that more airstrikes were imminent.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: Villages in southern Lebanon after strikes on Wednesday.

item.title: Residents Watch as Israeli War Planes Circle Over Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel has intensified its deadly military campaign against Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group, in recent days, striking targets across Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Looking out from a rooftop in Baysarieh as strikes target villages in southern Lebanon on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Max Bearak, Euan Ward, Luke Broadwater and Erika Solomon

item.title: Iran Threatens to Renew Strikes Against U.S. Amid Push for Diplomatic Deal
item.description: The warnings came after U.S. forces struck military sites in Iran. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Tuesday that negotiations to end the war were continuing.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Erika Solomon, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A crowd carried photos of Iran’s three supreme leaders during a ceremony in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Said to Have Observed Series of Iranian Threats Before Latest Strikes
item.description: Two U.S. officials said Iran launched drones near American ships, sent speedboats to mine the Strait of Hormuz and stepped up activity at some of its missile sites.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Monday.

item.title: Iran Begins Lifting Monthslong Internet Blackout, Officials Say
item.description: Organizations that track online traffic confirmed an uptick in activity on Tuesday, as Iranians reconnected to the global internet after a government-imposed shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: In Tehran on Sunday. The Iranian authorities imposed an internet blackout during the war with the United States and Israel.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Proposal Has Echoes of Half-Finished Gaza Deal
item.description: President Trump’s proposal to end the war with Iran would delay tackling the most contentious issues — a tactic that has had mixed results in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Palestinians living in tents among rubble in the Gaza Strip in February.

item.title: Israel Steps Up Lebanon Offensive as U.S. Pushes for Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group, has also kept up rocket and drone attacks on Israeli troops, as the escalation threatens to complicate diplomatic efforts.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rises after an Israeli air raid on the village of Yohmor in southern Lebanon on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Eric Schmitt, Erika Solomon and Euan Ward

item.title: The Wars in Ukraine and Iran Are More Alike Than You May Think
item.description: Aspects such as drone technology and diplomacy show how the wars intersect on the battlefield and in global alignments, providing a model for future conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Assessing the damaged buildings following Russian strikes in Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv, on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Carries Out Renewed Strikes in Southern Iran
item.description: Military officials said that the strikes targeted missile sites near a major Iranian port that threatened U.S. ships and planes.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships near Bandar Abbas, Iran, in May.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Israel Plans to Intensify Attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon
item.description: After the prime minister made the announcement, the Israeli military said it had struck more than 70 Hezbollah sites in the past day.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising in the city of Tyre, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Trump’s Pressure Had Little Effect on Iran’s Terms for a Peace Deal
item.description: President Trump had sought to force Tehran to accept American demands for a peace deal with a mixture of threats and military operations.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: For weeks, the Trump administration pursued a diplomatic agreement rather than returning to war in Iran.

item.title: Even After a Strait of Hormuz Deal, Moving 1,500 Ships Won’t Be Easy
item.description: An agreement to reopen the waterway would be followed by a complicated process of navigating a backlog of vessels stranded for nearly three months.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Companies will need to know how their ships will be prioritized if passage through the Strait of Hormuz is restored.

item.title: A deal with the U.S. would be preliminary and focused on ending the war, an Iranian official says.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands and Sanam Mahoozi
item.media:description: Esmail Baghaei, Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman, gives a news conference in Tehran earlier this month.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Ephrat Livni and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: Rubio Rebukes Hezbollah Chief Over Call for Lebanese to ‘Take to the Streets’
item.description: The militant leader, in a speech, had criticized the Lebanese government for engaging in direct talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A building hit by an Israeli strike in Tyre, Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Israel’s President, Isaac Herzog, Denounces Israeli Violence
item.description: In a speech, Isaac Herzog, whose role is largely ceremonial, delivered a grave indictment of settler attacks in the West Bank and abuse of prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: President Isaac Herzog of Israel in Panama this month.

item.title: Senate Republicans Cast Doubt on a Potential Peace Deal With Iran
item.description: Lawmakers, including some of President Trump’s closest allies, slammed the emerging agreement as effectively undermining the president’s own war goals.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Senator Thom Tillis said it was “questionable” that Iran would end its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rubio Says Details on Iran Nuclear Program Still to Be Negotiated
item.description: The Secretary of State said the United States was prepared to begin those talks if Iran opens the Strait of Hormuz as part of an apparently emerging deal on the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said nuclear talks with Iran may take place.

item.title: These Are 5 of the Main Issues to Be Resolved in an Iran-U.S. Peace Deal
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. and Iran could be close to a peace deal. The big issues at stake include the fate of Iran’s nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps at a government-organized demonstration in support of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, the new supreme leader, in April.

item.title: Rubio Says U.S.-India Ties Are Strong, Despite Fury Over Trump’s Actions
item.description: In New Delhi, Secretary of State Marco Rubio tried to explain why President Trump has pushed aggressive trade and immigration policies affecting India and Indians in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, at a news conference on Sunday in New Delhi, said the relationship between India and the United States “has not lost any momentum.”

item.title: Fate of War in Lebanon Rests Mostly With Outside Powers
item.description: A U.S.-Iran peace deal could calm the conflict in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. But that will depend mainly on what the United States, Iran and Israel decide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A building hit by an Israeli strike, as seen through a broken window at the Hiram Hospital in Tyre, Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Israeli Leader Says He and Trump Agree on Blocking an Iranian Nuclear Weapon
item.description: Analysts said Israel was worried that a potential agreement would not significantly degrade Iran’s nuclear and missile capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Marc Israel Sellem
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at a ceremony on the eve of Israel’s day of remembrance for fallen soldiers in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi and Julian Barnes

item.title: What to Know About the Potential U.S.-Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Iran and the United States appeared to be negotiating on a deal to end the war, but talks could take days. In the meantime, hostilities continue.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang, Amelia Nierenberg and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: People walking near a billboard depicting the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in Tehran, Iran, this month.

item.title: Iranian Officials Say Proposed Peace Deal Would Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran has not formally responded to President Trump’s announcement that a peace deal was close. But officials suggested the oil choke point in the Strait of Hormuz could reopen.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A crowd waving Iranian flags during a mass wedding ceremony in Tehran last week.
